body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

OpenAI Codex's TUI has a family of synchronous, local-only slash commands that render their response client-side without round-tripping to the model: `/status`, `/mcp`, `/help`, `/model` (the picker). Of these, `/status` is the cleanest no-LLM probe — it renders a panel with the active model, account, session id, and usage link, entirely in the Ink TUI.

The load-bearing transport fact (verified live against codex-cli 0.130.0): a synchronous slash command appends NOTHING to the codex rollout file. After a warm-up turn materialized the rollout (12 lines, ending in `event_msg/task_complete`), sending `/status` produced a delta of ZERO new rollout lines and the `task_complete` count stayed at 1. The status panel rendered in the TUI but no `user_message`, no `task_started`, and no `task_complete` were written.

This is a stronger guarantee than claudecode's. claudecode appends a `<local-command>` wrapper that its parser's `isLocalCommandContent` filter must discard; codex writes no transcript content at all for the slash, so there is simply nothing for the daemon to react to.

The spec's two Expected bullets:

**Bullet 1 — state stays `ready` throughout.** Because the slash writes no rollout line, the codex fswatcher sees no `transcript_activity`, the parser is never invoked, and no `state_transition` is emitted. The session sits in `ready` before, during, and after the slash.

**Bullet 2 — no `→ working` transition recorded for the slash command itself.** Direct consequence: a `working` transition fires only on the parser's first-activity force (`task_started` / assistant message) and ends on `task_complete` (parser.go maps `event_msg/task_complete` → `turn_done`). Neither appears for `/status`.

Live daemon evidence: the prod `irrlichd --record` daemon recorded exactly three state_transitions for the probe session — `ready` (new session created), `ready→working` (force ready→working on first activity), `working→ready` (agent finished turn → ready) — all driven by the single warm-up prompt. No fourth transition followed `/status` over the trailing window.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

The `/status` panel's text (model, account, usage) is rendered in the TUI but never appears as a rollout event, so the daemon has nothing to surface. This is correct: the scenario's premise is precisely that there is no model call to observe.

## Caveat on which commands count

Not every codex slash command is synchronous. `/compact` triggers a real model call (context summarisation — belongs to `context-compaction`). `/clear` (reset_session) and `/fork` mint a fresh rollout/session_id — they belong to session-reset / fork scenarios, not this one. The recipe restricts itself to `/status`, which is verified to be a pure client-side render.
caveats: `/clear` (reset_session) and `/fork` mint a fresh rollout with a new session_id — observable as a new session. Those belong to session-reset / fork, not this scenario. The recipe must avoid them.
`/compact` calls the model and would invalidate this scenario. Stick to `/status` (or `/mcp` / `/help`), which render entirely client-side.
If a future codex TUI change starts logging slash invocations into the rollout as real user/task events, this scenario would start failing — that is exactly the regression the recording locks down.

sources.note: Section 'Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)' — two Expected bullets (state stays ready; no working transition for the slash).
event_msg/task_complete maps to turn_done (155-172); only task_started / assistant activity forces ready→working. A slash that writes no rollout line drives neither.
Verified live: /status appended 0 lines to the rollout and produced 0 new state_transitions; status panel rendered client-side in the TUI only.
